content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed one amendment about tab-in-path parsing bug in assume_unchanged_paths. Verified the claim against src/adapters/worktree.rs:161-172 — split(\t).nth(1) does indeed truncate paths containing tab characters, since git ls-files -z outputs paths verbatim without quoting. This is a real correctness bug; the proposed splitn(2, ...) fix and regression test are appropriate.

## Votes

- `fr-7-5fb432a4`: Accept
  Verified in src/adapters/worktree.rs:161-172: after stripping the status byte and space, index_info has format `<mode> <hash> <stage>\t<path>`, and with -z mode git outputs the path verbatim (no quoting). A legal Git path containing a tab like `tab\tfile.txt` is split by `.split(b'\t')` into three pieces, and `.nth(1)` returns only the truncated `tab`, so remove_paths_from_index fails to remove the intended file and the checkpoint tree still includes the assume-unchanged content. The remediation (splitn(2, ...) to split at only the first tab, plus a regression test for a tab-in-path assume-unchanged file) is correct and tightly scoped.
